raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("association of puberty and youth alcohol use moderated by parenting styles")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

You must write at most 2 sentences: briefly say whether you agree (add one new reason) or disagree (give one counterpoint) based on evidence
State your answer choice clearly using \boxed{{letter}}
Based on the retrieved evidence, healthy family dynamics—including mutual cohesion and warm communication—are consistently linked to lower risks of adolescent substance use, including alcohol, while family dysfunction, conflict, and parental substance use are strongly correlated with elevated risk. Even though early puberty increases vulnerability to peer pressure and emotional distress, parental involvement acts as a critical buffer through emotional regulation, supervision, and modeling of responsible behavior. I agree with option A — parents do exert a moderating role in adolescent alcohol use during puberty. $\boxed{\text{A}}$
